analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet argues that despite a bleak anti-corruption history, a new commission is mandated by international and national law (not a donor fad), the EU has never blocked any investigation, and there is no alternative to functioning national institutions. Assessment by narrative: EU1 (successful story) – Not promoted; no reference to EU’s historic success or learning from past beyond a narrow procedural point. EU2 (united) – Not promoted; no mention of EU unity or solidarity among member states. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – Not promoted; the tweet is about anti-corruption frameworks and EU non-obstruction, not about EU policies improving citizens’ lives within the EU. EU4 (avant-garde actor) – Not promoted; no pioneering regulation claim. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Not promoted; while it notes the EU hasn’t blocked investigations, it doesn’t frame the EU as leading on democracy/human rights broadly. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Not promoted; no claim about global contributions, development, or multilateralism. Conclusion: None of the listed narratives are clearly promoted.
